symptoms.symptom_name: Multiple painful oral aphthous ulcers with odynophagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent aphthous ulceration is the defining mild mucocutaneous feature of Behcet's.

symptoms.symptom_name: Aphthous morphology without vesicles and no fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of preceding vesicles, gingivitis and fever argues against primary herpetic stomatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early dehydration — reduced dark urine and postural light-headedness, still swallowing saliva

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Concentrated low-volume urine and postural symptoms indicate early volume depletion; still-preserved saliva swallowing indicates it is not yet severe.

symptoms.symptom_name: No genital ulcers, ocular or neurological features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic involvement keeps this within the mild mucocutaneous phenotype.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical hydration reassessment (urine output, postural symptoms) after fluid trial
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Determines whether home oral rehydration is succeeding or face-to-face IV fluids are needed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods (FBC, U&E) if intake remains poor
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for AKI/electrolyte disturbance if dehydration persists; via GP/urgent care.
